base_url.description: Override the provider's API base URL. Empty (default) uses the SDK default endpoint. Use this to route through an OpenAI- or Anthropic-compatible proxy (LiteLLM proxy, Azure OpenAI, on-prem gateway, regional endpoint, ...). For OpenAI-compatible endpoints include the `/v1` path; for Anthropic-compatible endpoints stop at the host root (the SDK appends `/v1/messages`).

auth.description: Discriminated union selecting the credential source for the provider. See docs/api-key-helper.md for the full helper contract, examples, and recovery checklist.

command.description: Shell command whose stdout is the API key. Run via the configured `shell` (default bash). JSON `{"key":"...","expires_at":"<RFC3339>"}` is parsed strictly and memoized to a per-target cache file (early-refreshed via `refresh_margin_ms`). Plain non-JSON stdout is returned uncached and must be a single non-empty line.

shell.description: Shell that runs `command`. `bash` runs `bash -c <command>`. `powershell` runs `pwsh -Command <command>` when pwsh is on PATH (PowerShell 7+, cross-platform) and falls back to `powershell -Command <command>` (Windows PowerShell 5.1) otherwise. Default: bash.

refresh_margin_ms.description: Cache early-refresh threshold + minimum remaining TTL guard for fresh credentials, in milliseconds. Cache entries are stale once `now + margin >= expires_at`, forcing a helper refresh. Must be `>= 0`; `0` disables the guard. Default: 60000.

timeout_ms.description: Hard cap on one Resolve call (lock retries + helper exec), in milliseconds. Must be `> 0`. Default: 30000.

cache_key.description: Secret-free salt added to the cache fingerprint so an env / profile dependent helper (e.g. `aws sts ... --profile prod`) gets a separate cache file per profile. The value is used as-is; pull live env values in via the jsonnet `std.native('env')` / `std.native('must_env')` helpers.

path.description: Path to a file whose contents follow the same JSON / plain-string shape as the exec branch. Absolute, `~/`-prefixed, and relative paths are all accepted; relative paths resolve from the hook's working directory at fire time (not the directory of the config file). Omit the field to use the default $XDG_STATE_HOME/ccgate/<target>/auth_key.json; do not set it to an empty string. Read every hook fire (no caching).

refresh_margin_ms.description: Minimum remaining TTL guard for the file's `expires_at`, in milliseconds: a credential about to expire surfaces as `expired` instead of being handed to the SDK. `>= 0`; default 60000.

timeout_ms.description: Hard cap on the file read so a stalled mount surfaces as reason=timeout, in milliseconds. `> 0`; default 30000.
API timeout in milliseconds. 0 means no timeout. Default: 20000

log_path.description: Log file path. Supports ~ for home directory. Default: $XDG_STATE_HOME/ccgate/<target>/ccgate.log (target = claude / codex).

log_max_size.description: Max log file size in bytes before rotation. Default: 5242880 (5MB)

metrics_path.description: Metrics JSONL file path. Default: $XDG_STATE_HOME/ccgate/<target>/metrics.jsonl (target = claude / codex).

metrics_max_size.description: Max metrics file size in bytes before rotation. Default: 2097152 (2MB)

fallthrough_strategy.description: How to resolve LLM-driven fallthrough decisions. 'ask' (default): defer to Claude Code's prompt. 'deny': auto-deny uncertain requests; the hook message tells Claude that the decision was forced for safety and that it must not ask the user or bypass the restriction. 'allow': auto-approve uncertain requests; note that Claude Code's hook spec only delivers decision.message on deny, so Claude will NOT see any warning that an uncertain request was auto-approved — choose this only when you are comfortable with that. Only LLM fallthrough is affected (bypassPermissions, dontAsk, missing API key, non-Anthropic provider, and ExitPlanMode/AskUserQuestion still defer to Claude Code regardless).

append_allow.description: Allow guidance rules appended on top of the carried-over list (instead of replacing it). Typically used in project-local configs.

append_deny.description: Deny guidance rules appended on top of the carried-over list.

append_environment.description: Environment context appended on top of the carried-over list.
